question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full list of Juju hook events that a charm can observe:

## Lifecycle Hooks
| Hook | Ops Event | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `install` | `InstallEvent` | Fired once when the unit is first deployed |
| `config-changed` | `ConfigChangedEvent` | Fired when configuration values change (and after install) |
| `start` | `StartEvent` | Fired after install and initial config-changed |
| `upgrade-charm` | `UpgradeCharmEvent` | Fired when the charm code is upgraded |
| `stop` | `StopEvent` | Fired when the unit is about to be destroyed |
| `remove` | `RemoveEvent` | Fired when the unit is being removed |
| `update-status` | `UpdateStatusEvent` | Fired periodically (default every 5 minutes) |
| `collect-app-status` | `CollectStatusEvent` | Fired before status is reported (app level) |
| `collect-unit-status` | `CollectStatusEvent` | Fired before status is reported (unit level) |

## Leader Hooks
| Hook | Ops Event | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `leader-elected` | `LeaderElectedEvent` | Fired when this unit becomes the leader |
| `leader-settings-changed` | `LeaderSettingsChangedEvent` | Fired on non-leader units when leader settings change *(deprecated)* |

## Relation Hooks
*(fired per-relation, e.g. `database-relation-joined`)*
| Hook | Ops Event | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `relation-created` | `RelationCreatedEvent` | Fired when a relation is first established |
| `relation-joined` | `RelationJoinedEvent` | Fired when a new remote unit joins the relation |
| `relation-changed` | `RelationChangedEvent` | Fired when relation data changes |
| `relation-departed` | `RelationDepartedEvent` | Fired when a remote unit leaves the relation |
| `relation-broken` | `RelationBrokenEvent` | Fired when the relation is fully removed |

## Storage Hooks
*(fired per-storage, e.g. `data-storage-attached`)*
| Hook | Ops Event | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `storage-attached` | `StorageAttachedEvent` | Fired when storage is attached to the unit |
| `storage-detaching` | `StorageDetachingEvent` | Fired when storage is about to be detached |

## Container / Pebble Hooks (Kubernetes charms only)
*(fired per-container, e.g. `mycontainer-pebble-ready`)*
| Hook | Ops Event | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `pebble-ready` | `PebbleReadyEvent` | Fired when the Pebble agent in a workload container is ready |
| `pebble-custom-notice` | `PebbleCustomNoticeEvent` | Fired when a custom Pebble notice is recorded |
| `pebble-check-failed` | `PebbleCheckFailedEvent` | Fired when a Pebble health check starts failing |
| `pebble-check-recovered` | `PebbleCheckRecoveredEvent` | Fired when a Pebble health check recovers |

## Secret Hooks
| Hook | Ops Event | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `secret-changed` | `SecretChangedEvent` | Fired on observers when a secret's content is updated |
| `secret-remove` | `SecretRemoveEvent` | Fired on the owner when an old secret revision can be removed |
| `secret-rotate` | `SecretRotateEvent` | Fired on the owner when a secret's rotation interval elapses |
| `secret-expired` | `SecretExpiredEvent` | Fired on the owner when a secret revision expires |

## Action Hooks
| Hook | Ops Event | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `<action-name>` | `ActionEvent` | Fired when a user runs `juju run <action>` on the unit |

## Series / OS Upgrade Hooks *(deprecated)*
| Hook | Ops Event | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | `PreSeriesUpgradeEvent` | Fired before the machine OS series is upgraded |
| `post-series-upgrade` | `PostSeriesUpgradeEvent` | Fired after the machine OS series is upgraded |

---

**Total: ~30 distinct hook types** (many are multiplied by the number of relations, storage mounts, containers, actions, or secrets defined in `metadata.yaml` / `charmcraft.yaml`).

In the **ops framework**, you observe these in `__init__` like:
```python
self.framework.observe(self.on.install, self._on_install)
self.framework.observe(self.on.database_relation_joined, self._on_db_joined)
self.framework.observe(self.on.mycontainer_pebble_ready, self._on_pebble_ready)
```
